orcas-island-tomorrow-forecast.content:
**🏝️ Orcas Island Forecast – Friday, Feb 20**

**Highs near 40°F**, mostly cloudy with light SE winds 3-6 mph.

**⛴️ Ferry Travel:** Excellent conditions expected. Calm winds all day—a significant improvement from Thursday's gusty 25 mph gusts. Smooth crossings likely.

**🥾 Outdoor Activities:** Good hiking weather but dress in layers for temps hovering around 40°F. Mostly dry until late evening.

**⚠️ Evening Note:** Mixed rain/snow showers possible after 4am (30% chance). Minimal accumulation expected. Plan to return before late night if weather-sensitive.

**Bottom line:** Ideal day for island exploration!
orcas-island-tomorrow-forecast.rawContent:
## 🌤️ Tomorrow's Forecast for Orcas Island, WA (Friday, Feb 20)

### Temperature
| | |
|---|---|
| **High** | 40°F |
| **Low** | 37°F |

### Conditions
- **Daytime:** Mostly cloudy, light southeast wind 3-6 mph
- **Evening:** A chance of rain and snow showers, mainly after 4am. East wind 6-8 mph

### Precipitation
- **Chance:** 30% (primarily after 4am)
- **Type:** Mixed rain and snow showers possible overnight
- **Snow accumulation:** Little or no accumulation expected

### Wind Conditions (Island Travel)
- **Daytime:** Light winds, SE 3-6 mph — **favorable for ferry travel**
- **Overnight:** East wind 6-8 mph — calm conditions

### ⚓ Marine Advisory
Conditions look calm for Friday. However, note that **today (Thursday)** has stronger winds with NNE 10-15 mph and gusts to 25 mph, which could affect ferry crossings. By Friday, winds will subside significantly.

**Overall:** Good travel day for island visits, but carry layers as temps will hover around 40°F. Watch for possible mixed precipitation Friday night if returning late.

seattle-tomorrow-forecast.content:
**Seattle Tomorrow (Fri, Feb 20)**

**Morning:** Mostly cloudy with calm winds. Dry conditions, low around 36°F.

**Afternoon:** Continued mostly cloudy skies with light south winds around 5 mph. High near 43°F.

**Evening:** Slight chance of rain showers developing before 10pm, increasing to 30% chance of mixed rain and snow showers overnight. Little to no snow accumulation expected.

wa-road-conditions.content:
# Washington State Road Conditions
**February 19, 2026**

## I-90 Snoqualmie Pass
**⚠️ CAUTION** - Open with winter conditions. Snow showers likely (60%), temps 12-27°F. Chains required.

## SR-20 North Cascades Highway
**🔴 CLOSED** - Seasonal closure at MP 134 (Ross Dam Trailhead). Reopens April/May.

## Cascade River Road
**🔴 CLOSED** - Flooding/landslides at MP 21.

## Stehekin Valley Road
**🔴 CLOSED** - Landslides since December.

## I-5 Seattle Corridor
**✅ CLEAR** - No incidents reported. Normal traffic patterns.

## SR-20 to Anacortes
**✅ CLEAR** - Open to ferry terminal. Ferries operating normally; reservations recommended.

## Ferry Alerts
- **⚠️ DELAYS** - Kingston terminal construction through July; expect 20-30 min delays
- Seattle/Bremerton 10:15pm sailing canceled Feb 20

---
*Check WSDOT real-time map before travel: wsdot.com/travel/real-time/map*
wa-road-conditions.rawContent:
I have gathered substantial information. Let me now compile a comprehensive road conditions report based on all the data collected.

---

# 🚗 Washington State Road Conditions Report
**As of February 19, 2026**

---

## ❄️ I-90 SNOQUALMIE PASS

**Weather Forecast (Snoqualmie Pass area):**
- **Today**: Snow showers likely (60% chance), high ~27°F, wind chill 18-23°F. Less than 1" daytime accumulation expected. Patchy fog 10-11am.
- **Tonight**: 10% chance snow after 4am, low ~12°F, patchy dense fog
- **Friday-Sunday**: Continued snow chances (40-80%), temperatures 21-35°F, accumulations possible
- **Next Week**: Snow likely through Wednesday, temperatures 27-36°F, mixed rain/snow Sunday-Monday

⚠️ **Advisory**: Winter driving conditions expected. Carry chains and check WSDOT real-time map before travel.

---

## 🚧 HIGHWAY 20 (North Cascades Highway)

### **🔴 CLOSED (Partial)**
- **Status as of 2/18/2026**: SR-20 is **CLOSED at Milepost 134 (Ross Dam Trailhead)**
- **Reason**: Seasonal winter closure due to snow and avalanche hazards
- **Expected Reopening**: Typically April or early May, weather-dependent
- **High Point**: Washington Pass at 5,477 feet elevation

### Related Road Closures:
- **Cascade River Road**: **CLOSED** at Johnson Cabin (MP 21) due to flooding and landslides. Foot/bicycle traffic allowed beyond gate.
- **Stehekin Valley Road**: **CLOSED** due to landslides as of 12/10. Do not travel to Stehekin area.

### Open Access Roads:
- Thornton Lake Road: OPEN (rough conditions, high clearance recommended)
- Sibley Creek/Hidden Lake Road: OPEN (unmaintained, expect snow, 4WD needed)
- Silver/Skagit Road to Hozomeen: OPEN to US border (gate at Hozomeen closed; no border crossing)

---

## 🛣️ I-5 SEATTLE CORRIDOR

- No major closures or incidents reported from available sources
- Standard commute traffic patterns apply
- Check [WSDOT Real-Time Map](https://wsdot.com/travel/real-time/map) for live traffic flow

---

## ⛴️ SR-20 TO ANACORTES (San Juan Islands Ferry Terminal)

**Road Status**: SR-20 from I-5 to Anacortes Ferry Terminal is **OPEN** for travel

### San Juan Islands Ferry Service (Anacortes):
- Ferries operating on normal schedule between Anacortes and San Juan Islands (Lopez, Shaw, Orcas, Friday Harbor)
- **Reservations recommended** for vehicle travel

### System-wide Ferry Alerts:
| Alert | Details |
|-------|---------|
| **Card Payment Fee** | Starting March 1, 2026 - 3% fee on all card transactions |
| **Seattle/Bremerton** | 10:15pm sailing from Bremerton canceled Feb 20 for refueling |
| **Kingston Terminal** | Construction through July 2026; 20-30 min delays Tue-Thu on 9:40pm sailing |
| **Kingston Area** | SR-104 weekday roadwork delays (traffic light installation project) |
| **Colman Dock** | Interior construction through early April |

---

## 📋 SUMMARY

| Route | Status | Notes |
|-------|--------|-------|
| **I-90 (Snoqualmie Pass)** | ⚠️ OPEN - Winter Conditions | Snow expected, carry chains |
| **SR-20 (North Cascades)** | 🔴 CLOSED | Seasonal closure at MP 134 |
| **I-5 (Seattle)** | ✅ OPEN | Normal conditions |
| **SR-20 to Anacortes** | ✅ OPEN | Ferry service operating normally |

---

**Resources:**
- WSDOT Real-Time Map: https://wsdot.com/travel/real-time/map
- WSF Alerts: https://wsdot.com/ferries/schedule/bulletin.aspx
- NPS North Cascades: https://www.nps.gov/noca/planyourvisit/road-conditions.htm

travel-summary.content:
**TRAVEL ADVISORY: Seattle → Orcas Island**
**Friday, February 20, 2026**

---

**Overall Recommendation: GOOD TRAVEL DAY** ✅

Conditions are favorable for your Seattle to Orcas Island trip. Clear roads, calm waters, and dry weather through the afternoon make this an ideal window for travel.

**Route Status:**
- I-5 and SR-20 to Anacortes: Clear, no incidents
- Anacortes-Orcas Ferry: Operating normally, calm seas (3-6 mph winds)

**Weather:** 36-43°F, mostly cloudy, dry until evening. Mixed rain/snow develops after 10 PM in Seattle, after 4 AM on Orcas.

**Recommended Departure:** 7:00 AM - 12:00 PM from Seattle. Avoid departures after 3:00 PM due to evening weather deterioration.

**Action Items:**
- Book ferry reservations immediately (per WSDOT)
- Pack warm layers, rain gear, and waterproof footwear
- Bring phone charger for travel updates

**Alerts:**
- Evening mixed precipitation expected—plan to arrive before nightfall
- Kingston terminal experiencing delays (does not affect your Anacortes route)
- Snoqualmie Pass requires chains (not on your route)

**Bottom Line:** Depart by late morning for optimal conditions. Roads are clear, ferries running smoothly, and you'll beat the evening weather change. An excellent day for island travel.

---
*232 words*
travel-summary.rawContent:
# 🚗⛴️ Seattle → Orcas Island Travel Advisory
**Friday, February 20, 2026**

---

## 1️⃣ Current Conditions (Seattle)
| Metric | Value |
|--------|-------|
| Temperature | 36°F (feels like 28°F) |
| Sky | Overcast, 92% humidity |
| Wind | NNW at 11 mph |

---

## 2️⃣ Tomorrow's Travel Outlook

### 🚗 Seattle → Anacortes Drive (~80 mi)
- **I-5 Corridor:** ✅ **CLEAR** - No incidents, normal traffic
- **SR-20 to Anacortes:** ✅ **CLEAR** - Open to ferry terminal
- **Conditions:** Mostly cloudy, 36-43°F, dry until evening

### ⛴️ Anacortes → Orcas Island Ferry
- **Conditions:** ✅ **EXCELLENT** - Calm winds (3-6 mph), smooth crossings expected
- **Status:** Ferries operating normally

### 🏝️ Orcas Island
- **High:** ~40°F, mostly cloudy, light winds
- **Activities:** Great hiking weather

---

## 3️⃣ Recommended Departure Time

| Option | Leave Seattle | Why |
|--------|--------------|-----|
| **Best** ⭐ | 7:00-9:00 AM | Maximize daylight; avoid evening weather change |
| Acceptable | Up to 12:00 PM | Still dry; allows afternoon island time |
| **Avoid** | After 3:00 PM | Evening rain/snow showers developing |

**Book ferry reservations ASAP** — recommended by WSDOT.

---

## 4️⃣ What to Pack/Prepare

| Item | Reason |
|------|--------|
| 🧥 Warm layers | 36-43°F temps |
| ☔ Rain jacket/umbrella | Evening showers possible |
| 🥾 Waterproof footwear | Wet trails likely |
| 🔋 Phone charger | Ferry/road updates |
| ☕ Hot drinks/snacks | Cold waits possible |

---

## 5️⃣ Alerts & Concerns

| Priority | Alert |
|----------|-------|
| ⚠️ **WATCH** | Mixed rain/snow showers developing after 10 PM in Seattle, after 4 AM on Orcas |
| ℹ️ **NOTE** | Kingston terminal delays (20-30 min) — *doesn't affect Anacortes route* |
| 🚫 **AVOID** | I-90 Snoqualmie Pass — chains required, 60% snow chance *(not on your route)* |

---

## ✅ Bottom Line

**Excellent travel day!** The route is clear, ferries are running smoothly, and weather is dry until late evening. Depart by late morning, plan to return or settle in before nightfall, and you'll have an ideal island trip.
